topic: Theme 2: Political and Artistic Culture in the Spanish-speaking World
subject: spanish

items.q: How do you say 'I study English at school' in Spanish?
items.misconception: Estudio el inglés a la escuela.
items.why: Students add unnecessary articles or wrong prepositions. Languages as school subjects DROP the article: 'estudio inglés', 'me gusta el inglés' (general likes need article). Location: EN (not a) for places: en el colegio, en casa, en clase.

items.q: Which mood follows 'quiero que' (I want that)?
items.why: Students use indicative after que. Expressions of wish, emotion, doubt, or impersonal expressions trigger SUBJUNCTIVE: 'Quiero que VENGAS' (not 'que vienes'). Different subjects require que + subjunctive.
Students apply English pronunciation. Spanish H is ALWAYS silent. 'Hola' is 'OH-lah', not 'HO-lah'. (The breathy sound students associate with H comes from j or g+e/i in Spanish.)

items.q: How many diputados sit in the Congreso?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 350. “230” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which is the future of 'votar' for 'él'?
items.why: Correct answer: votará. “votó” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year was the current Spanish Constitution adopted?
items.why: The real figure is 1978. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
